standardization_map.colloquial: tree mein jo upper wala node hai

concepts.name: Tree (Data Structure)
concepts.description: A non-linear data structure that simulates a hierarchical tree structure, with a root value and subtrees of children with a parent node, represented as a set of linked nodes.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.description: The topmost node in a tree data structure, from which all other nodes descend.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.description: A fundamental entity in a tree data structure, representing an element that can store data and have connections to other nodes.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.description: A link or connection between two nodes in a tree, representing their relationship.

concepts.name: Parent Node
concepts.description: A node that has one or more child nodes directly connected below it in the hierarchy.

concepts.name: Child Node
concepts.description: A node that is directly connected to another node above it, which is called its parent node.

concepts.name: Family Tree Example
concepts.description: A common real-world analogy used to explain the hierarchical structure and relationships (parent-child) within a tree data structure.

concepts.name: Level of a Node
concepts.description: The distance of a node from the root node, with the root typically at level 0.

prerequisites.rationale: Understanding the general concept of a Tree (Data Structure) is necessary to define its constituent elements like Nodes.
To grasp the Family Tree as an example, one must first have a conceptual understanding of what a Tree data structure represents.
The Family Tree example is used by the speaker to demonstrate and introduce the concept of individual nodes ('दादाजी', 'पापा', 'बेटे').
The Family Tree example clearly illustrates the concept of a Parent Node through 'दादाजी'.
The Family Tree example clearly illustrates the concept of a Child Node through 'बेटे'.
A Parent Node is a specialized type of Node, so understanding the general Node concept is essential.
A Child Node is a specialized type of Node, so understanding the general Node concept is essential.
Edges are defined as connections between Parent and Child Nodes, requiring prior understanding of the Parent Node.
Edges are defined as connections between Parent and Child Nodes, requiring prior understanding of the Child Node.
The Root Node is a specific type of Node that has unique properties, thus requiring prior knowledge of what a Node is.
The Level of a Node is defined by its distance from the Root Node, making understanding the Root Node crucial.
